extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ attacked before the final twists of the category 1 summit finish after KUSS shelled the group, taking both the stage and red jersey by 5 seconds over CARAPAZ.
tactical_patterns: KUSS paced so hard on final climb that GC contenders couldn't contest KOM points even if they wanted, gifting him polka dots.
ROGLIČ attacked just before technical finish twists, exploiting hesitation between CARAPAZ and MARTIN who played cat-and-mouse instead of chasing immediately.
Jumbo-Visma placed 5 riders in top 10 on a mountain stage, demonstrating tactical depth rendering individual GC rivals helpless.
EF's double GC crash (MARTÍNEZ early, WOODS on narrow pinch zone) reveals vulnerability on wet narrow roads before key climbs.

rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: paced final climb so violently that he won KOM by default while working, suggesting untapped stage-winning potential when unleashed
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: attacked tactically before finish twists rather than on steep gradient, showing race craft beyond pure power
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: chased ROGLIČ for second but hesitated initially in cat-and-mouse with MARTIN, suggesting strategic uncertainty without team support
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: struggled on descent and couldn't follow on final climb, already appearing to pack it in on stage 1
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: negative: crashed on narrow pinch zone mid-race, eliminated from stage win contention despite being pre-race favorite
rider_signals.MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe: neutral: remounted after early crash and rejoined via 6.5-mile chase with OWEN, ankle complaint but finished
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: took white jersey 50 seconds ahead of second-placed young rider, nearly as impressive as ROGLIČ in GC demolition
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: lost over a minute on final climb, dropped from key GC group
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: had a hard day, losing over a minute with VALVERDE
